title: Scope file-changed stale_conditions to the cited line range instead of whole-file hash
description: Add an optional lines field to the file-changed stale_conditions schema branch (research.schema.json, additive, 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0, identity migration -- no data transform needed). Add a new computeFileLineRangeHash helper (packages/pi-context/src/content-hash.ts) that parses a comma-separated lines spec (e.g. '483-492,597-614,651-652'), extracts and hashes only those lines' content. Wire it into block-api.ts's stampDeclaredBaselines and context-sdk.ts's evaluateStalenessCandidates: when a file-changed condition carries lines, use the line-scoped hash; when absent, keep the existing whole-file hash unchanged. State the residual limitation explicitly in code comments and at closure: this is line-number-based, not content-anchored, so an unrelated edit earlier in the file that shifts line numbers can still mis-fire -- a smaller-scope version of the prior imprecision, not a total fix. Also correct R-0030's own live-fired stale_conditions entries (the triggering instance): add lines from its sibling citations[] entries where available (5 of its 7 file-changed entries have a matching citations[] lines value) and re-stamp baseline_hash under the new algorithm via the sanctioned write path (update-block-item), never a direct edit. Fixes FGAP-137.
acceptance_criteria: research.schema.json's file-changed branch declares an optional lines field (type string, no pattern, matching the existing citations[].lines/evidence[].lines convention); schema version bumped 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0 with an identity migration registered.
computeFileLineRangeHash exists, correctly parses comma-separated N and N-M segments, and produces a deterministic hash over only the referenced lines' content.
stampDeclaredBaselines and evaluateStalenessCandidates both use the line-scoped hash when lines is present on a file-changed condition, and the unchanged whole-file hash when it is absent.

Runtime demo (load-bearing, not just R-0030 looking clean): a fresh file-changed condition with lines set, an edit OUTSIDE that range does NOT fire the condition, an edit INSIDE that range DOES fire it.

R-0030's stale_conditions updated through update-block-item: the 5 entries with a matching citations[] lines value carry that lines value and a freshly-stamped baseline_hash under the new algorithm; the 2 entries with no matching lines are left as whole-file conditions, unchanged. R-0030's status remains complete (not transitioned to stale) -- the substantive findings were never in question.
npx tsx scripts/check-context-currency.ts and pi-context context-validate --json no longer report R-0030 as a staleness-candidate.
npm run build + npm run check + npm test (+ check-changelog + parity-check + check-config-schema) all exit 0.
